senses.definition: the process of building outwards or advancing a coastline or geological layer by deposition
examples: The map shows the progradation of the shoreline over thousands of years.
Progradation of deltaic shores occurred before subsidence altered the coast.

senses.definition: plural of progradation; outward build-up or outward growth in geology or geography
examples: The report discusses multiple progradations along the coast.
Deltas show several layers of progradations across different time periods.

senses.definition: moving forward; advancing in the same direction as orbital motion or along a geological front
examples: The prograde motion of the rock strata indicates forward deposition.
In astronomy, prograde orbits rotate in the same sense as most planets.

senses.definition: to advance forward or to deposit forward in geological terms
examples: Sediment progrades as the delta grows outward.
The shoreline progrades under rising sea levels.

senses.definition: deposited forward in the past; advanced outward as sediment accumulated
examples: The coastline prograded after the river changed its course.
Deposits prograded and formed new shoreline features.

senses.definition: deposits forward or advances outward, especially of sediments or shoreline features
examples: The delta progrades during the wet season.
River deposition progrades the shore over time.

senses.definition: the act of depositing sediments outward; the process of advancing a coastline or geological front
examples: Prograding deltas create new land by accreting sediments.
Prograding shorelines can change local habitats.

senses.definition: a plan of activities or a sequence of events; a set of instructions for a computer
examples: I wrote a little program to organize my files.
The TV program starts at 8 p.m.

senses.definition: to create a plan or set of instructions; to schedule activities
examples: She programmed the device to turn on at sunrise.
The software is programmed to save automatically.

senses.definition: capable of being programmed or configured to perform specific tasks
examples: This device is programable and can be customized.
A programable thermostat adjusts temperature automatically.

senses.definition: relating to or characteristic of a program or programs; may be used to describe a planned approach
examples: A programatic approach to learning can be effective.
The policy was criticized as overly programatic.

senses.definition: set up with a program or instructions to operate in a specified way
examples: The robot is programmed to sort items by size.
A programmed routine helps you manage time.

senses.definition: a person who writes computer software; commonly spelled programmer
examples: The programer fixed several bugs in the app.
Programers often work with multiple programming languages.

senses.definition: plural of programer; multiple people who write computer software
examples: The team includes several programers from different countries.
Programers collaborate to build the new app.

senses.definition: the act or process of writing computer programs; more commonly 'programming'
examples: Programing requires logical thinking and problem solving.
He specialized in programing for embedded systems.

senses.definition: plural of programming efforts or modules; rarely used in standard form
examples: The project includes several programings to test different features.

senses.definition: a misspelling or rare variant of 'programist' meaning a programmer or someone who programs; standard form 'programmer' or 'programmer' in many dialects
examples: The programist debugged the code.

senses.definition: acting as part of a planned program or policy; done in a deliberate, controlled way
examples: The policy was presented in a programmatic way.
Programmatic controls reduce risk.

senses.definition: an archaic or nonstandard form of 'program' in some languages; not common in modern English
examples: The word 'programma' appeared in older texts.

senses.definition: the qualities of being programmable; the potential to be programmed
examples: The programmabilities of the device allow for extensive customization.

senses.definition: the ability of a system to be programmed or configured to perform tasks
examples: The programmability of the microcontroller is impressive.
High programmability makes the software adaptable.

senses.definition: able to be programmed or set to behave in a specific way
examples: This timer is programmable to run at different times.
A programmable keyboard lets you customize keys.

senses.definition: things that can be programmed; devices or features with programmable capabilities
examples: The market includes many programmables like smart plugs and sensors.

senses.definition: a misspelling or nonstandard form of 'programmer'; not a standard English term
examples: The text used 'programmar' instead of 'programmer'.

senses.definition: plural of programma (rare/archaism); historically used to mean programs or plans
examples: Ancient texts mention the programmata of the guild.

senses.definition: see earlier sense under 'programmatic' (duplicate entry)
examples: See previous entry for programmatic.

senses.definition: in a way that is done by a program or by following a plan
examples: The device is programmatically configured to run tests.
Data is processed programmatically rather than manually.
